text: Think of the cache as a tiny goblin hoard that keeps grabbing repeated data.
The retry loop is a little gremlin that keeps poking the service until it wakes up.
Your memory leak behaves like an ogre eating heap space after every request.
The scheduler sends another tiny creature into the queue for every missed job.
The cache stores repeated data so future reads can complete with lower latency.
The retry loop sends another request after a transient failure or timeout.
A memory leak increases heap usage when allocated objects remain reachable.
The scheduler places missed jobs back into the queue for later execution.
